commands.repair_reason: - [P2] Manual review is needed because the remaining decision is accepting a security-sensitive Mistral credential-boundary change and waiting for checks, not an automated code repair.; Needs attention: The patch is narrowly filtered and documented, but it intentionally changes a Mistral credential boundary and needs maintainer acceptance before merge. (sha=b50e21e8bbb61954d112ccdd1909101bce83b455)
commands.review_summary:
The PR forwards filtered `csrftoken` plus `ory_session_*` cookies to Mistral Vibe usage, adds `console.mistral.ai` to Mistral cookie import domains, and updates docs/tests for that boundary.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection shows current main sends only `csrftoken` to the Mistral Vibe endpoint and omits `console.mistral.ai` from auto-import, while the PR body shows redacted csrf-only failure and csrf plus `ory_session_*` success.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Manual review is needed because the remaining decision is accepting a security-sensitive Mistral credential-boundary change and waiting for checks, not an automated code repair.

target.title: fix(mistral): restore ory_session cookie for vibe fetch, add console.mistral.ai to import domains
target.body:
## Bug Monthly Plan metric (introduced in #1635) stopped working after `08e19e386d83` — the app shows API spend instead of subscription usage percentage regardless of the selected metric. ## Root cause `08e19e386d83` (steipete's "fix: isolate Mistral console cookies", part of the #1635 merge) refactored the vibe fetch as a security improvement, stripping the request down to only `Cookie: csrftoken=`. The intent was correct (keep admin.mistral.ai cookies origin-bound), but `console.mistral.ai` requires the `ory_session_*` session cookie in addition to the CSRF token to authenticate the request. Without it the endpoint returns 302, which `fetchOptionalVibeUsage` silently swallows and returns `nil` — so `extraRateWindows` is never populated and the Monthly Plan window never appears. ```sh $ curl 'https://console.mistral.ai/api-ui/trpc/billing.vibeUsage?batch=1&input=%7B%220%22%3A%7B%22json%22%3Anull%2C%22meta%22%3A%7B%22values%22%3A%5B%22undefined%22%5D%2C%22v%22%3A1%7D%7D%7D' \ -H 'Accept: */*' \ -H 'Accept-Language: en-GB,en-US;q=0.9,en;q=0.8' \ -H 'Cookie: csrftoken=;' 302 Found

302 Found

``` A second issue: `MistralCookieImporter.cookieDomains` did not include `console.mistral.ai`, meaning the `csrftoken` cookie (which may be set under that subdomain by the console frontend) was never imported in auto mode. This caused `csrfToken` to be `nil`, which short-circuits the vibe call entirely before it even starts. ## Fix **`MistralUsageFetcher.fetchVibeUsage`** — accepts an optional `cookieHeader` and forwards a filtered cookie string to `console.mistral.ai` containing: - `csrftoken=` (required for CSRF validation) - any `ory_session_*` cookies extracted from the source header (required for session auth) ```sh $ curl 'https://console.mistral.ai/api-ui/trpc/billing.vibeUsage?batch=1&input=%7B%220%22%3A%7B%22json%22%3Anull%2C%22meta%22%3A%7B%22values%22%3A%5B%22undefined%22%5D%2C%22v%22%3A1%7D%7D%7D' \ -H 'Accept: */*' \ -H 'Accept-Language: en-GB,en-US;q=0.9,en;q=0.8' \ -H 'Cookie: csrftoken=; ory_session_coolcurranf83m3srkfl=""; ' [{"result":{"data":{"json":{"usage_percentage":4.518170666666666,"quota_changed_this_month":false,"payg_enabled":false,"reset_at":"2026-07-01T00:00:00Z"}}}}] ``` All other admin.mistral.ai cookies are excluded — the security intent of the original refactor is preserved; only the minimum set needed by the console endpoint is forwarded. **`MistralCookieImporter.cookieDomains`** — added `console.mistral.ai` so the `csrftoken` cookie is captured during auto-import from browser cookies. ## Why this approach The `ory_session_*` cookie is issued by Ory Kratos for `*.mistral.ai` and is valid across all Mistral subdomains by design. Forwarding it to `console.mistral.ai` doesn't expand the credential boundary beyond what the browser would do when a logged-in user visits the console. The alternative — scoping a separate console cookie import path — would require a new settings field and import flow with no meaningful security gain over filtering the existing cookie. ## Test plan - [x] Manual cookie source with full cookie header: verify Monthly Plan shows usage % - [x] Vibe API failure (invalid token): verify app falls back to spend display, no crash - [x] Pay-as-you-go: verify spend display unaffected Closes #1721
target.author: lfmundim
